title: biscutella montana var. genuina
intro: Biscutella montana var. genuina, also known as Mountain Biscutella, is a plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its yellow flowers and hairy stems. Traditionally, Mountain Biscutella has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues and wounds. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving coughs, and soothing skin irritations, making it a valuable asset in traditional herbal medicine. Mountain Biscutella can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or tea, and its roots and leaves can be used in herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
